beats.activity: Wake up, make strong espresso, check weather and plan the day ahead.
beats.reasoning: Viktor wakes to clear skies and heat—a shift from yesterday's rainy mood. Strong espresso ritual before heading out; moderate energy (50/100) means a slower morning is fine.

beats.activity: Drive from Leschi to Georgetown clinic via surface streets; grab water for the hot day ahead.
beats.reasoning: Surface-street commute is familiar after yesterday's SR-520 closure workaround. Heat and smoke from BC wildfires mean hydration is already on his mind.

beats.activity: Morning and early-afternoon PT sessions; mix of runners and post-op rehab clients.
beats.reasoning: Steady clinic day; Viktor is in his element managing pain and progress. No work events interfere; he stays focused on clients in the heat.

beats.activity: Lunch at a shaded indoor spot; grab a cold drink and sandwich to beat the heat.
beats.location_name: Molly Moon's Homemade Ice Cream (Georgetown, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Familiar lunch spot from recent days; the heat (91F) and smoke make indoor dining appealing. Ice cream and a cool environment help reset before afternoon sessions.

beats.activity: Afternoon PT sessions; final clients of the day.
beats.reasoning: Solid afternoon block; Viktor finishes his workday by 5pm, leaving time for evening plans before the heat peaks further.

beats.activity: Drive home to Leschi; stop for water and light snacks.
beats.reasoning: Early commute home to avoid peak heat hours and prepare for evening outdoor concert. Heat and smoke mean he wants time to refresh before heading out.

beats.activity: Quick shower, change into comfortable clothes, grab a light dinner or snack.
beats.reasoning: Cool down after the hot day; prepare for the evening outdoor concert. Light meals align with his preference for intentional eating on hot days.

beats.activity: Drive to Gas Works Park for the free outdoor concert; find a spot with good sightlines and shade if available.
beats.reasoning: Viktor is drawn to good weather and outdoor experiences with close friends or solo vibe. The concert starts at 6pm; he arrives early to settle in as heat cools toward evening.

beats.activity: Enjoy the outdoor concert at Gas Works Park; soak in the clear evening, watch the music and city lights.
beats.reasoning: Perfect alignment with Viktor's night-owl nature and love of being outdoors on clear days. Smoke is moderate and evening temps are comfortable. The big crowd is balanced by his solo comfort and the open-air vibe.

beats.activity: Drive back home to Leschi; reflect on the evening under clear skies.
beats.reasoning: Late-evening drive home; Viktor's night-owl rhythm means he's energized by the concert and comfortable heading home after 10pm.

beats.activity: Unwind at home with a sketchbook or light painting; wind down for the night.
beats.reasoning: Post-concert creative time; Viktor's rhythm includes studio time as a way to process the day. Keeps energy steady before sleep.
7+ hour sleep window ending well before 6am next day, as required. Night owl Viktor stays up later but still gets solid rest before another workday.

diary:
Started the day with that strong espresso ritual—needed it before heading to Georgetown. The heat was already creeping in during my commute, so I grabbed extra water. Morning sessions were solid; a couple runners with IT band issues and one post-op knee rehab who's finally progressing. Molly Moon's was packed at lunch, but the AC and a cold sandwich saved me from the worst of it.

Afternoon flew by. By the time I locked up the clinic, I could feel that 91-degree weight settling over the city. Drove home, showered off, then headed straight to Gas Works. The park was already filling up when I arrived, but I found a decent shady spot near the old industrial structures. The concert was exactly what I needed—clear skies, good music, and that view of the city lights kicking on as dusk fell. Even the wildfire smoke didn't ruin it. Drove back feeling lighter than I have in weeks.
thought_process: I leaned into the heat today instead of fighting it. Normally I'd skip evening plans on a hot day, but seeing the forecast—clear, no rain threat—made me think it was worth getting out. The clinic was routine, which was actually grounding after a chaotic week with staffing issues. I skipped my usual post-work gym session because I knew the concert would be better for my headspace. The outdoor event felt like a gift on a day like this, and I prioritized that over productivity. The wildfire smoke was a minor irritant in the back of my mind, but not enough to deter me. By evening, I realized I needed this kind of reset more than I admitted to myself.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
